heading: Trade agreement negotiating authority
label: 2902 Trade agreement negotiating authority

children.label: A
children.text: before , may enter into trade agreements with foreign countries; andJune 1, 1993

children.label: i
children.text: such modification or continuance of any existing duty,

children.label: ii
children.text: such continuance of existing duty-free or excise treatment, or

children.label: iii
children.text: such additional duties;

children.label: A
children.text: reduces any rate of duty (other than a rate of duty that does not exceed 5 percent ad valorem on ) to a rate which is less than 50 percent of the rate of such duty that applies on ; orAugust 23, 1988August 23, 1988

children.label: B
children.text: increases any rate of duty above the rate that applies on .August 23, 1988

children.label: A
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the aggregate reduction in the rate of duty on any article which is in effect on any day pursuant to a trade agreement entered into under paragraph (1) shall not exceed the aggregate reduction which would have been in effect on such day if a reduction of 3 percent ad valorem or a reduction of one-tenth of the total reduction, whichever is greater, had taken effect on the effective date of the first reduction proclaimed in paragraph (1) to carry out such agreement with respect to such article.

children.label: B
children.text: No staging under subparagraph (A) is required with respect to a rate reduction that is proclaimed under paragraph (1) for an article of a kind that is not produced in the United States. The United States International Trade Commission shall advise the President of the identity of articles that may be exempted from staging under this subparagraph.

children.label: A
children.text: the difference between the reduction without regard to this paragraph and the next lower whole number; or

children.label: B
children.text: one-half of 1 percent ad valorem.

No reduction in a rate of duty under a trade agreement entered into under subsection (a) on any article may take effect more than 10 years after the effective date of the first reduction under paragraph (1) that is proclaimed to carry out the trade agreement with respect to such article.

A rate of duty reduction or increase that may not be proclaimed by reason of paragraph (2) may take effect only if a provision authorizing such reduction or increase is included within an implementing bill provided for under and that bill is enacted into law.section 2903 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: unduly burdens or restricts the foreign trade of the United States or adversely affects the United States economy; or

children.label: B
children.text: the imposition of any such barrier or distortion is likely to result in such a burden, restriction, or effect;

children.label: i
children.text: the reduction or elimination of such barrier or other distortion; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the prohibition of, or limitations on the imposition of, such barrier or other distortion.
A trade agreement may be entered into under this subsection only if such agreement makes progress in meeting the applicable objectives described in .section 2901 of this title

children.heading: Bilateral agreements regarding tariff and nontariff barriers
children.label: c Bilateral agreements regarding tariff and nontariff barriers

children.text: Before , the President may enter into bilateral trade agreements with foreign countries that provide for the elimination or reduction of any duty imposed by the United States. A trade agreement entered into under this paragraph may also provide for the reduction or elimination of barriers to, or other distortions of, the international trade of the foreign country or the United States.June 1, 1993

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no trade benefit shall be extended to any country by reason of the extension of any trade benefit to another country under a trade agreement entered into under paragraph (1) with such other country.

children.label: A
children.text: the agreement makes progress in meeting the applicable objectives described in ;section 2901 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: such foreign country requests the negotiation of such an agreement; and

children.label: i
children.text: provides written notice of such negotiations to the Committee on Finance of the Senate and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives, and

children.label: ii
children.text: consults with such committees regarding the negotiation of such agreement.
The 60-day period of time described in paragraph (3)(C) shall be computed in accordance with .section 2903(e) of this title

In any case in which there is an inconsistency between any provision of this Act and any bilateral free trade area agreement that entered into force and effect with respect to the United States before , the provision shall not apply with respect to the foreign country that is party to that agreement.January 1, 1987

children.heading: Consultation with Congress before agreements entered into
children.label: d Consultation with Congress before agreements entered into
A
children.text: the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Finance of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: each other committee of the House and the Senate, and each joint committee of the Congress, which has jurisdiction over legislation involving subject matters which would be affected by the trade agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: the nature of the agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: how and to what extent the agreement will achieve the applicable purposes, policies, and objectives of this title; and

children.label: C
children.text: all matters relating to the implementation of the agreement under .section 2903 of this title

If it is proposed to implement two or more trade agreements in a single implementing bill under , the consultation under paragraph (1) shall include the desirability and feasibility of such proposed implementation.section 2903 of this title

children.heading: Special provisions regarding Uruguay Round trade negotiations
children.label: e Special provisions regarding Uruguay Round trade negotiations

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding the time limitations in subsections (a) and (b), if the Uruguay Round of multilateral trade negotiations under the auspices of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade has not resulted in trade agreements by , the President may, during the period after , and before , enter into, under subsections (a) and (b) of this section, trade agreements resulting from such negotiations.May 31, 1993May 31, 1993April 16, 1994

children.heading: Application of tariff proclamation authority
children.label: 2 Application of tariff proclamation authority
children.text: No proclamation under subsection (a) to carry out the provisions regarding tariff barriers of a trade agreement that is entered into pursuant to paragraph (1) may take effect before the effective date of a bill that implements the provisions regarding nontariff barriers of a trade agreement that is entered into under such paragraph.

children.heading: Application of implementing and “fast track” procedures
children.label: 3 Application of implementing and “fast track” procedures
A
children.text: in applying subsection (a)(1)(A) of to any such agreement, the phrase “at least 120 calendar days before the day on which he enters into the trade agreement (but not later than ),” shall be substituted for the phrase “at least 90 calendar days before the day on which he enters into the trade agreement,”; andsection 2903 of this titleDecember 15, 1993

children.label: B
children.text: no provision of subsection (b) of other than paragraph (1)(A) applies to any such agreement and in applying such paragraph, “;” shall be substituted for “;”.section 2903 of this titleApril 16, 1994June 1, 1991

children.heading: Advisory committee reports
children.label: 4 Advisory committee reports
children.text: The report required under regarding any trade agreement provided for under paragraph (1) shall be provided to the President, the Congress, and the United States Trade Representative not later than 30 days after the date on which the President notifies the Congress under of his intention to enter into the agreement (but before ).section 2155(e)(1) of this titlesection 2903(a)(1)(A) of this titleJanuary 15, 1994
